findings.title: IPv4-mapped IPv6 path skips BLOCKED_HOSTS re-check, reopening metadata-IP SSRF bypass
findings.problem: The new IPv4-mapped check re-tests the mapped address only against is_link_local/is_loopback/is_unspecified/is_multicast, but NOT against BLOCKED_HOSTS. BLOCKED_HOSTS is enforced only by the string comparison at line 77 (`hostname in BLOCKED_HOSTS`). For an IPv4-mapped literal the hostname string is e.g. `::ffff:100.100.100.200`, which does not string-match any BLOCKED_HOSTS entry, so line 77 passes. `100.100.100.200` (Alibaba Cloud metadata) is a plain public IP: it is not loopback/link-local/unspecified/multicast, so both line 82 (outer IPv6) and line 87 (mapped IPv4) pass. Verified: `http://[::ffff:100.100.100.200]` is ACCEPTED, defeating the metadata block that D-10 and this hunk exist to enforce. (169.254.169.254 happens to also be link-local so it is still caught, but 100.100.100.200 relies solely on the BLOCKED_HOSTS string match.)
findings.fix_hint: also block when str(mapped) is in BLOCKED_HOSTS (and/or when mapped.is_private per policy) so mapped forms of BLOCKED_HOSTS entries like 100.100.100.200 are rejected
findings.why_it_matters: An attacker-supplied *arr URL using an IPv4-mapped IPv6 literal reaches cloud metadata endpoints that BLOCKED_HOSTS is specifically meant to protect, re-opening the SSRF hole this change set out to close.
source_window: if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:
findings.canonical_line_content: if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:

findings.title: SSRF bypass via NAT64 well-known prefix (64:ff9b::/96) embedding blocked IPv4 addresses
findings.intent_doc_match: Diff comment references 'D-10' -- a design decision to block IPv4-embedded-in-IPv6 SSRF bypasses (the same class this finding is in), so this is a direct miss of the diff's own stated goal.
findings.problem: The diff adds detection for IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (::ffff:0:0/96 prefix) via addr.ipv4_mapped, but does not handle the NAT64 'Well-Known Prefix' 64:ff9b::/96 (RFC 6052), which also encodes an arbitrary IPv4 address directly in the low 32 bits. Python's ipaddress.IPv6Address.ipv4_mapped only recognizes the ::ffff:0:0/96 form and returns None for 64:ff9b::/96 addresses, so the new check does not fire, and none of is_link_local/is_loopback/is_unspecified/is_multicast on the outer IPv6Address catch it either (verified: is_loopback is False and is_global is True for 64:ff9b::127.0.0.1). Verified directly against validate_arr_url: validate_arr_url('http://[64:ff9b::127.0.0.1]:8080/') and validate_arr_url('http://[64:ff9b::169.254.169.254]/latest/meta-data/') both return (True, '') -- loopback and the cloud metadata IP are allowed through when expressed via the NAT64 prefix even though their plain IPv4 forms are blocked. On hosts/containers with NAT64 enabled (common in IPv6-only or dual-stack cloud/container networking), traffic to these addresses is actually routed to the embedded IPv4 destination, giving a real SSRF path to loopback services or the cloud metadata endpoint.
findings.fix_hint: Generalize the embedded-IPv4 extraction beyond ipv4_mapped: for an IPv6Address, also check the 64:ff9b::/96 NAT64 prefix (and consider 6to4 2002::/16) by extracting the trailing 32 bits as an IPv4Address and re-running the same is_link_local/is_loopback/is_unspecified/is_multicast checks against it, mirroring the pattern already used for ipv4_mapped.
findings.why_it_matters: This is exactly the SSRF class the diff is trying to close (per the D-10 reference) -- an attacker who controls the *arr URL can use an alternate IPv6 embedding of a blocked IPv4 address to reach loopback services or the 169.254.169.254 cloud metadata endpoint on any deployment where NAT64 is present, silently bypassing the newly-added protection.
source_window: if addr.is_link_local or addr.is_loopback or addr.is_unspecified or addr.is_multicast:
# Check IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (e.g., ::ffff:127.0.0.1) per D-10
findings.canonical_line_content: if addr.is_link_local or addr.is_loopback or addr.is_unspecified or addr.is_multicast:

findings.title: IPv4-mapped IPv6 bypasses explicit metadata IP blocklist
findings.problem: The new IPv4-mapped IPv6 branch only checks address classes, not the explicit BLOCKED_HOSTS set. I verified `validate_arr_url("http://[::ffff:100.100.100.200]")` returns `(True, "")` while `http://100.100.100.200` is blocked. Since `100.100.100.200` is intentionally listed as a cloud metadata address, any downstream HTTP stack that accepts IPv4-mapped IPv6 can bypass the SSRF guard.
findings.fix_hint: After `mapped = addr.ipv4_mapped`, reject `str(mapped) in BLOCKED_HOSTS` before or alongside the mapped address-class checks, and add a regression test for `http://[::ffff:100.100.100.200]`.
findings.why_it_matters: 100.100.100.200 is intentionally listed as a cloud metadata address, so any downstream HTTP stack that accepts IPv4-mapped IPv6 can bypass the SSRF guard.
source_window: # Check IPv4-mapped IPv6 addresses (e.g., ::ffff:127.0.0.1) per D-10
if mapped.is_link_local or mapped.is_loopback or mapped.is_unspecified or mapped.is_multicast:
